title: pseuderanthemum kewense
intro: Pseuderanthemum kewense, also known as the Kew mint or False Hygrophila, is a tropical flowering plant species native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Pseuderanthemum kewense is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
